title: Cyathocephalum schmidtii
taxonomy: Cyathocephalum schmidtii is a species of flowering plant in the family Compositae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This classification is based on its characteristic flower head, which resembles those found in the Compositae family. More specifically, Cyathocephalum schmidtii is a member of the subfamily Asteroideae and the tribe Senecioneae. Within the Senecioneae tribe, it is placed in the genus Cyathocephalum, which is closely related to other genera such as Senecio and Brachyglottis. Further classification may also involve the family level, where it is placed in the Compositae family, which is a part of the larger asterid clade.

intro: Cyathocephalum schmidtii, also known as Schmidt's Cyathocephalum, is a plant species belonging to the family Compositae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, providing potential health benefits for treating various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cyathocephalum schmidtii include flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
